title: Sprouts Chaat
description: Zingy evening snack of mung sprouts, onion, tomato, lemon, and chaat masala. High protein, low fuss.
explanation: Sprouts chaat is the default healthy Indian snack when you want something crunchy and spicy without deep frying. Homegrown or store-bought mung sprouts both work.
Steam sprouts lightly if raw sprouts are hard to digest for you; keep them raw for maximum crunch if your stomach tolerates them.
instructions: Rinse sprouts. Optionally steam 2–3 minutes and cool.
Combine sprouts with onion, tomato, cucumber, and chilli.
Add lemon, chaat masala, roasted cumin, and salt. Toss well.
Garnish with coriander and pomegranate. Serve immediately.
stepNotes: Light steaming makes sprouts easier to digest without killing all the bite.
Chaat masala is salty—taste before adding more salt.
Skip onion for Jain; add extra cucumber, tomato, and hing-based seasoning.
Best eaten fresh; assemble just before serving for crunch.
A handful of crushed roasted peanuts adds calories and texture.

variationSections.title: Chaat upgrades
items: Add boiled potato cubes for a fuller snack.
Drizzle thin mint chutney and tamarind chutney.
Mix in boiled kabuli chana for extra protein.

variationSections.title: Meal roles
items: Light lunch with a roti on the side.
Travel box without chutney (add lemon packets instead).
